extraction.one_line_summary: BARGUIL Warren bridged to a leading group containing QUINTANA and CONTADOR, sat on while they chased, then outkicked them for the Bastille Day win.
tactical_patterns: FROOME Chris attacked repeatedly on steepest gradients of final climb then descended aggressively, probing ARU without teammates present.
LANDA Mikel went in early break for SKY, forcing other GC teams to chase rather than SKY defending yellow.
BARGUIL Warren sat on QUINTANA and CONTADOR's chase work, exploiting their need to close gap while conserving energy.
MARTIN Dan paced relentlessly on final climb, pulling back 45 seconds on leaders in final 3km for GC group.

rider_signals.FROOME Chris: positive: attacked on steepest gradients and descended aggressively despite appearing to suffer earlier, confirming form deception tactics work
rider_signals.ARU Fabio: positive: matched all FROOME attacks and descended competently without any teammates, but complete team isolation remains critical vulnerability
rider_signals.BARGUIL Warren: positive: tactical maturity to recognize sitting on QUINTANA/CONTADOR chase was optimal path to stage win on Bastille Day
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: positive: went in early break for SKY, offensive team tactic forcing other GC squads to chase instead of SKY defending
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: positive: rebounded from previous day's struggles to finish second, showing resilience after appearing defeated
rider_signals.CONTADOR Alberto: positive: attacked from weakened position throughout tour, found form to finish third despite struggling for GC earlier
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: positive: pulled back 45 seconds on leaders in final 3km through sustained pacing, key domestique work for GC group
